feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Integration Suite
feed.subtitle: SAP Integration Suite Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How to extract Equipment Type assigned to Load Requisitions from SAP IBP via ODATA API
entry.content:
Hello Community Members,

We are trying to extract information relevant to SAP IBP's Transportation Load Building Load Requisitions. We need to extract the Load Requisition Orders and Equipment type assigned to each load in an excel/CSV file.

We tried exploring the various APIs available such as:

https://api.sap.com/api/IBP_Purchasing_RAP_ODataService/overview

https://api.sap.com/api/IBP_Snapshot_RAP_ODataService/overview

https://api.sap.com/package/IBPAPIDetailedPeggingService/odatav4

https://api.sap.com/api/IBP_Keyfigure_ODataService/overview (This is only available for Time Series)

 

Can you help us in identifying any API that can be used to extract Equipment Type assigned to Load Requisitions from SAP IBP? or if the OBP Data Sources like STD_MALAMOT_EQUOM can be accesses via any APIs?

Thanks,

entry.title: Uniqueness constraint violation - SAP CPI transport
entry.content:
Hi

We have the 1 non-production and 1 production environments  for SAP Cloud Integration

Dev and test packages are created in the non-prod tenant . Flows created in dev package are moved to the corresponding test package. From test packages flows are transported to Production tenant using TMS . 

For changes in the iflow ,

change done in DEV → versioned
delete artefact in TEST
moved flow in test and deployed

When transporting the flow from test to production CPI tenant(using TMS) get the uniqueness violation.
TEST → PROD → uniqueness violation

Had referred the guided answer which refers to delete the package  and reimport  in the tenant.

Guided Answers

Does it mean that everytime , a change is incurred , Package in production needs to be deleted and reimported? 

Package id in the overview screen remains the same across the tenants.

Can you please check to advise ?

Thanks

Anusha

entry.title: Integration Suite Authentication
entry.content:
Hi,

I have completed the Integration Suite Mission with the trial account and then continued setup the real integration of our api within our respective subaccount. Everything is setup and now I am not able to authenticate against the API. Our API has an API-Key mechanism and I want to disable all auth or simplify the auth on the SAP side of things. In the mission there was a booster setting everything up. In reality this is not really documented in an obvious way.

I am now running into the following error testing the api:

<!doctype html><html lang="en"><head><title>HTTP Status 401 – Unauthorized</title><style type="text/css">body {font-family:Tahoma,Arial,sans-serif;} h1, h2, h3, b {color:white;background-color:#525D76;} h1 {font-size:22px;} h2 {font-size:16px;} h3 {font-size:14px;} p {font-size:12px;} a {color:black;} .line {height:1px;background-color:#525D76;border:none;}</style></head><body><h1>HTTP Status 401 – Unauthorized</h1></body></html>

How can I assign the ESBMessaging.send Role? I tried setting up a service instance but cannot get the OAuth flow to work since I cannot create OAuth2 Credentials inside the  SAP Process Integration Runtime looking the one from the mission. 

JSON View of the Service Key to be created is missing token url, clientID, clientSecret....

{
    "key-type": "client_id_secret",
    "X.509": "",
    "certificate-pinning": true,
    "validity": 365,
    "key-length": 2048
}

Ideal solution would be to completely skip AUTH on this side of things since our api is secured.

entry.title: Stop Calling APIs for Every Currency Conversion - with BTP Advanced Event Mesh
entry.content:
If you've been spending time on the SAP Community lately, you may have noticed a little trend: several really well-written blog posts showing how to build currency conversion flows in SAP Integration Suite. They're solid learning examples - Postman sends a JSON payload, an iFlow calls an external exchange rate API, some transformations happen, and the result arrives in a nicely formatted email.

I love seeing this kind of hands-on content. And I want to add a friendly "but wait..." - because I think there's a fundamentally different way to think about this problem. One that removes most of the complexity instead of elegantly managing it.

Let me show you what currency conversion looks like through an Event-Driven Architecture lens, using SAP Advanced Event Mesh (AEM).

What the iFlow approach is really doing

Let's be honest about the architecture behind those integration flows. Here's the typical pattern:

  1. A caller (Postman, an app, a system) sends a request: "give me EUR to USD"
  2. The iFlow wakes up, validates the input, calls an external API (RapidAPI, ECB, etc.)
  3. The result goes through a series of transformations: JSON → XML → XSLT → HTML
  4. The converted amount lands in someone's inbox as a formatted email

Every single step in that chain exists for one reason: the systems involved don't speak the same language, and the integration layer mediates between them - synchronously, on every request.

That's not a bug. That's what Integration Suite was designed for. But it does come with a cost.

The synchronous integration tax

Look at those iFlow steps again - not from an implementation perspective, but from a business logic perspective:

  • Groovy script to validate JSON → not business logic. It's a workaround for the fact that there's no contract between caller and receiver.
  • Content modifier to extract parameters → not business logic. It's format negotiation.
  • JSON-to-XML conversion → definitely not business logic. Pure translation overhead.
  • XSLT to generate HTML → presentation logic that has no business being in a middleware layer.
  • Mail adapter → the result is delivered to a human inbox, which is a dead end for any downstream system that might also need that rate.

None of this is wrong. But every single one of these steps is a symptom of tight coupling - and tight coupling is exactly what we're trying to move away from in modern integration architecture.

The event-driven alternative

Now let's rethink this from scratch with AEM.

The key question is: does currency conversion actually need to be request-driven?

Exchange rates don't change because someone asked for them. They change because the market moves. So why are we building an architecture that fetches them on demand - potentially hundreds of times a day, from multiple systems - when we could publish them once and let every interested party receive them instantly?

Here's what the event-driven approach looks like:

Step 1: A single Rate Publisher

A lightweight service (a Cloud Foundry app, a BTP function, even a scheduled job) fetches the latest exchange rates from your source API on a schedule - or reacts to rate-change webhooks if the provider supports them. It then publishes a structured event to AEM:

json
{
  "specversion": "1.0",
  "type": "fx.rate.updated",
  "source": "ecb.europa.eu",
  "time": "2025-04-09T08:00:00Z",
  "data": {
    "from": "EUR",
    "to": "USD",
    "rate": 1.082,
    "validFrom": "2025-04-09T00:00:00Z"
  }
}

Published to a topic: fx/rates/EUR/USD

That's it. One publish. One API call.

Step 2: Every consumer subscribes

Any system that needs exchange rates - invoicing, procurement, treasury, reporting, AI agents - subscribes to fx/rates/> (the wildcard catches all currency pairs). They receive the event the moment it's published. No polling. No HTTP round trips. No waiting.

Your SAP S/4HANA system gets it. Your custom finance app gets it. Your AI agent gets it. All simultaneously. All from the same single upstream API call.

Step 3: Consumers own their format

Here's where the mapping steps disappear - not because we got better at mapping, but because we removed the reason mapping was needed in the first place.

When you define a clean event schema once and every participant agrees on it upfront, there's nothing to translate in the middle. A Java consumer reads JSON natively. An ABAP consumer does its own deserialization. A Python AI agent processes the event directly. The broker doesn't care about format - and neither does the publisher.

No JSON-to-XML. No XSLT. No Groovy validation. Those steps were artifacts of tight coupling, and tight coupling is gone.

But what about reliability?

The iFlow approach has one hidden fragility: if the external exchange rate API is down, the integration fails - and every caller gets an error. You can build retry logic into the iFlow, but it only helps the current caller.

With AEM:

  • The Rate Publisher handles retries independently of any consumer
  • AEM supports guaranteed message delivery - if a consumer is temporarily offline, it receives the event when it comes back
  • Last-value retained messages mean a new subscriber always receives the most recent valid rate immediately on connect, even if no new rate has been published since it started

Your invoicing system reconnects after a maintenance window at 2 AM? It gets the latest rate instantly. No missed events, no stale data.

And what about the email?

The email output in those iFlow demos is charming - and totally understandable for a learning exercise. But think about what it represents: a conversion result delivered to a human inbox, where it stops being useful to any downstream system.

In an event-driven world, the converted amount is data in motion. It doesn't go to an inbox - it feeds back into the mesh. A treasury dashboard subscribes to consumption events and visualizes rates in real time. An AI agent subscribes and evaluates whether a rate threshold should trigger a hedging action. A reporting service aggregates daily rates without ever making an API call.

The email is the end of a pipeline. The event is the beginning of an ecosystem.

Putting it together: Sense → Think → Act

This is where it gets really exciting - especially when you bring agentic AI into the picture.

With AEM as the backbone, currency rate data flows through a Sense → Think → Act loop:

  • Sense: AEM receives rate update events from the publisher and distributes them across all connected systems
  • Think: An AI agent (via a SAP Agent or any LLM-powered service) evaluates the rate in the context of open purchase orders, hedging thresholds, or FX exposure — and decides whether action is needed
  • Act: The agent publishes a command event — trigger a hedge, create an FX booking, send a targeted alert — without any human in the loop

This is not something you can bolt onto an iFlow. It requires a fundamentally different architecture: one where data flows continuously, systems are decoupled, and intelligence can be inserted at any point in the mesh.

The bottom line

  Integration Suite iFlow SAP Advanced Event Mesh
API calls per rateOne per requestOne per rate change
Consumers servedOne callerUnlimited subscribers
Mapping stepsSeveral (JSON↔XML, XSLT, etc.)None — schema defined once
Failure impactCaller gets errorPublisher retries independently
New consumer addedNew iFlow or modificationJust subscribe
AI/agent readyNoYes — natively

The iFlow currency conversion demos on this community are great teaching material — they genuinely are. But when you're thinking about production architecture, ask yourself: am I solving the problem, or am I solving the problem of not having the right foundation?

SAP Advanced Event Mesh is that foundation. And once it's in place, "currency conversion" stops being an integration project and becomes a single subscription.

Happy connecting — the event-driven way! 🚀

entry.title: Architectural Guidance: Implementation of CI/CD Best Practices for SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
We are currently in the PoC phase of implementing a CI/CD pipeline for SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration) and are evaluating the SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery service. Before moving to a production-grade implementation, we require expert clarification on the following points to align with SAP’s recommended best practices:

1)Strategic Value Proposition: Beyond standard automation, what are the primary architectural justifications for implementing a CI/CD pipeline specifically for BTP Integration Suite in an enterprise landscape?

2)The "XML Readability" Gap: A major pain point in the CI/CD workflow is that iFlows are stored in GitHub as XML files. This makes code reviews, change tracking, and "diffing" extremely difficult for developers compared to viewing changes directly in the SAP CPI UI. Does SAP have a recommended strategy or toolset to make Git-based iFlow reviews more human-readable?

3)Comparison of Error Mitigation: We currently use cTMS, which effectively reduces manual errors and provides a structured transport path. Aside from automation speed, what are the critical technical advantages of a CI/CD pipeline that cTMS cannot provide?

4)Impact on Developer Experience: Moving to CI/CD adds the complexity of managing Git branches and Pull Requests.Considering the friction created by managing Git branches for iFlows, how does the CI/CD approach specifically enhance developer productivity?

entry.title: Security material in shared tenant (Cloud Integration)
entry.content:
Hi team, 

We are using shared BTP integration suite-Cloud Integration across different teams.. We have central service

entry.title: Security material - shared BTP Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

We're using the shared BTP Integration Suite - Cloud Integration across different teams within our organization. We have a central service team managing topics like security material creation and management. Currently, different teams follow the SNOW process for this, but some teams are complaining that the process is complicated due to the lack of a shared process, requiring SNOW incidents for everything.Some teams are asking if others face the same issue. I understand that managing things ourselves is easier than raising SNOW incidents, but is this a significant pain point or a rare occurrence (like once a month)?Could you guide me on governance for this?

entry.title: sap apim timeout issue
entry.content:
We are using SAP APIM and we get gateway timeout after a minute +/-

We are requesting toi increase it up to 5 minutes.

Many thanks

entry.title: SPROXY shows “Service outdated” only for newly created Integration Suite Service Interfaces
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

we are currently testing ABAP proxy generation with SAP Integration Suite.

We observed the following behavior in transaction SPROXY:

  • Migrated PI/PO service interfaces can be activated and do not show this issue
  • New service interfaces created directly in SAP Integration Suite can also be used to generate ABAP proxies
  • Runtime communication works: the ABAP proxy can receive messages from Integration Suite
  • However, in SPROXY these newly created interfaces remain in status “Service outdated”
  • Regeneration and activation of the proxy do not remove the status

 

So the issue seems to occur only for service interfaces modeled natively in Integration Suite, not for interfaces migrated from PI/PO.

Questions:

  1. Is this a known limitation or bug of ABAP proxy generation with newly created Integration Suite service interfaces?
  2. Can the “Service outdated” status be ignored if runtime communication is already working?
  3. Has anyone managed to get rid of this status for newly created interfaces?

Any experience would be greatly appreciated.

KR

Stefan

entry.title: How to change the SAP Business One Microsoft 365 integration client secret？
entry.content:
The client secret on my Microsoft Azure has been changed, but I couldn't find where to modify it in SAP Business One.

 

Caused an error when I tried to rebind my Outlook account

 

Thank you all for your replies！

entry.title: TPM to send 997 converts to status idoc and back to SAP
entry.content: From B2B partner we are receiving 997 functional acknowledgements to CPI , but we don't find any option to route this through mapping to the ECC system. Any inputs will be highly appreciated.Thanks

entry.title: Request for Bulk Download of Failed Message Attachments – Salesforce_Satmetrix_Survey_I28
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team,

We have an interface in CPI (Salesforce_Satmetrix_Survey_I28) where we are currently observing a high number of failures. The business has requested us to download all failed messages for further analysis.

At present, we are logging the source payload and HTTP response body from Satmetrix as attachments. The current requirement from the business is to download these attachments in bulk and share them for analysis.

Could you please assist with a way to extract and download these attachments in bulk at the earliest?

Please let us know if any additional details are required from our side.

Thanks & regards,
Eani sree.

entry.title: Integration suite provisioning errors out
entry.content:
During provision process, while activating the capabilities, it errors out.

Graph capability failed.

Error P007: Activation of Data Space Integration capability has failed. To resolve, see SAP Note 

DCC-IS-BUILD  - https://dcc-is-build.integrationsuite.cfapps.eu22.hana.ondemand.com/shell/home

Hyperscale - Azure

entry.title: What tends to create the most complexity in your SAP integrations today?
entry.content:
As you read in the caption, maybe Data, APIs, or process orchestration? Lately it feels like SAP integrations get complicated faster than expected. What starts simple ends up having a lot more moving parts.

In your experience, what’s usually the biggest source of that complexity?

entry.title: Please indicate how to change the name of the Job Information label from English to Spanish
entry.content:
Please indicate how to change the name of the Job Information label from English to Spanish.

We have searched and validated, but we have not found a way to change that label. Please attach the following image, as it refers to the documentation requested by the manufacturer. See image.

entry.title: Reprocess OData bulk records
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I'm trying to post 1000 records into s/4 system using odata v2 adapter (batch mode) and suppose I see connection issue after processing 500 records then from 501th record and beyond them all the records gets stopped and goes into exception and flow falls into failed status. again we have to reprocess and duplicates will be into s/4. How do we reprocess from 501th records automatically if such connection issue occurs?

I'm facing timeout issue as 1000 records are not been processed in 10min. I had to rerun and duplicates are sent but overwritten luckily still wanted to check if that's not happening in s/4!

entry.title: Best Practices for JWT Validation and JWKS Caching in SAP API Management using KVM
entry.content:
Hi everyone,
I’m designing a JWT validation flow in SAP API Management for an integration project. The goal is to keep the proxy environment‑agnostic while ensuring high performance through JWKS caching. I’d appreciate feedback on the approach and any potential improvements.

Current Flow Summary:

  1. Validate the request body to ensure it is well‑formed JSON.
  2. Remove sensitive headers (e.g., Cookies) before further processing.
  3. Read the IdP base URL from an environment‑specific KVM.
  4. Normalize the URL using JavaScript for service callouts.
  5. Look up JWKS in cache.
  6. Call the IdP’s .well-known/jwks.json endpoint if the cache is empty.
  7. Cache the JWKS (TTL: 120 seconds).
  8. Verify JWT (RS256, scopes).
  9. Validate the issuer claim against the KVM value.

    Are there alternative or recommended ways to further externalize configuration (beyond KVMs) while keeping the proxy fully environment‑agnostic?

    Looking forward to your insights on optimizing this zero‑touch setup.

    Best regards,
    Somya

entry.title: EXCEPTION SEGMENT UNKNOWN RAISED error while calling RFC IDOCTYPE_READ_COMPLETE in Biztalk Middlewar
entry.content:
Hello Mates,

Good Day!

Currently we are facing issue when importing the extended idocs from SAP S4 to biztalk with IDOC segment release 701. We tried changing the WE20 outbound partner profiles parameter idoc release to 701 and also in T-code WE82 but no luck. All segments are in released stated and unlocked and also verified AUTH Issue.

I highly doubt the feasibility and compatability if SAP S4 actually accepts 701 release or not?!!!

Flow:

SAP S4 (idoc extension with 701 release)  -> MS Biztalk -> EDI Partners

Error:

Error while retrieving or generating the WSDL. Adapter message: Details: ErrorCode=RFC_EXCEPTION. ErrorGroup=RFC_ERROR_APPLICATION_EXCEPTION. SapErrorMessage=EXCEPTION SEGMENT_UNKNOWN RAISED.  AdapterErrorMessage=Error returned by RfcCallReceiveEx while calling RFC: IDOCTYPE_READ_COMPLETE..

 

Screenshot:

Thanks and Regards,

Rajesh PS

entry.title: Unable to Assign Collection Management Attributes via API in S/4HANA Public Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

We are currently working on an integration between our S/4HANA Public Cloud system and a Microsoft Dynamics 365 system for customer creation and updates.

As part of this integration, we are using the API_BUSINESS_PARTNER inbound service along with its related entities such as A_BusinessPartner, A_BusinessPartnerAddress, A_BusinessPartnerContact, A_BPRelationship, A_BPContactToAddress etc

During the process, we are creating the following roles for each customer:

  • FLCU00 (Customer – Financial Accounting)
  • FLCU01 (Customer – Sales Area)
  • UDM000 (Collections Management role)

While the roles are successfully created, we are facing challenges with Collections Management data. Specifically, we are unable to assign the following attributes to the customer via the integration:

  • Collection Segment
  • Collection Group
  • Collection Strategy
  • Collection Specialist

We have not been able to identify any API or entity within the Business Partner service that supports maintaining these collections-related attributes.

During our analysis, we found several SAP-delivered jobs, such as:

  • Assignment of Collection Profiles to Business Partners
  • Changing Segment Data
  • Replacing Collection Specialists

However, these jobs do not fully meet our requirements. For example, the Assignment of Collection Profiles to Business Partners job requires a fixed “Created On” date parameter. This makes it difficult to automate daily processing, as we cannot dynamically define a rolling date range (e.g., “today” or “today - 1”) when scheduling the job.

As a result, we are struggling to design an automated solution that ensures Collections Management attributes are consistently assigned to newly created customers.

We would greatly appreciate any guidance on:

  • Available APIs or extensibility options to maintain Collections Management data
  • Recommended best practices for automating this process in S/4HANA Public Cloud
  • Any alternative approaches that others have successfully implemented

Our goal is to ensure that our Accounts Receivable team can effectively use the Process Receivables app with complete and accurate customer data, including assigned collection profiles and contacts.

SAP Integration Suite SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition SAP S/4HANA business partner #

entry.title: I need total CPI IP Ranges for PROD & QA system.
entry.content: I need total CPI IP Ranges for  PROD & QA system.
